results.note: Shipped in PageToMovie.Engine AmbientSfxClassifier (PromptVersion=v2_grounded). Tightens empty cases (speech/parentheticals), no invented beds, layer rules from blind review + score variance analysis.

samples.visual: Bagheera (purring).
Narrator speaks.
Bagheera (dry).
Bagheera speaks.
Machua Appa speaks.
Bagheera (aloud).
Mowgli (shouting).
Bagheera speaks.
Mowgli slides down a trunk, angry and indignant.
Billy speaks.
Moonlight turns the plain milky white. Far behind, temple bells bang and conches blare. Messua's distant weeping carries on the wind.
High above, gray shapes of the BANDAR-LOG watch, whisper, invent a plan among themselves. Below, Mowgli walks ashamed between Bear and Panther.
The whine becomes a humming purr from every quarter-then the full-throated aaooughh:
Petersen Sahib (smiling under his mustache).

He sits by the gate. A VILLAGE MAN comes out. Mowgli stands, opens his mouth, points down it-food. The man stares and runs shouting up the single street. PRIEST-big, fat, white clothes, red-and-yellow mark on his forehea…
Seals swim in a long dark line, heading toward summer beaches. Their voices rise together-a deep-sea song, sad as a national anthem.

Full moon. A hilltop of stones and boulders. AKELA, great gray Lone Wolf, lies full length on his rock. Below him, forty or more wolves of every size and color. Cubs tumble in the center. Senior wolves pad up, inspect, r…
Morning. Big Toomai scolds while Little Toomai hangs his head.
An old SEA LION shrugs. A WALRUS grumbles of ice only. At last Kotick meets the great lazy SEA COW-manatee-like, slow, wise in currents-who leads him without words under a cliff.
Mowgli looks up at the stars. He and Akela move at a steady pace. Gray Brother joins them. The great striped hide is rolled into a bundle on Mowgli's head.
Mowgli speaks.
Narrator speaks.
Father Wolf at the mouth. Mother Wolf lifts her head.
Vixen creeps under the waterproof to NARRATOR. The animals settle. Rain softens. Far off, a bugle practices for the morning.
Dark water. Kotick follows the Sea Cow through a long drowned tunnel beneath the cliffs, lungs burning, until-

Hot sun. A small draggled mongoose lies senseless on the middle of a garden path-fur like a little cat, head and habits like a weasel, eyes and restless nose pink. TEDDY, a small English boy, stands over him. TEDDY'S MOT…
Bagheera speaks.

A native clerk at a table pays wages. Drivers take their pay and join the line of elephants ready to start. Catchers and hunters lean on guns and laugh at the plains drivers. PETERSEN SAHIB lies along the back of his cle…
Narrator speaks.
Little Toomai speaks.
Lean English officer-type man, mid-30s to 40s, period moustache, shirt-sleeves under a waterproof coat; angular face, weathered outdoor skin, short neat hair.
Adult lean gray Indian wolf, male, rangy muscle under coarse gray coat, pale underbelly, amber eyes, long muzzle, alert ears.
Great gray she-wolf (Raksha), heavy maternal build, big gray nose, thick ruff, green-reflecting eyes, powerful shoulders.
Four small gray wolf cubs, soft puppy coats, oversized paws and ears, dark muzzle masks, bright curious eyes.
Indian jackal, smaller than the wolves, dusty scruffy coat, bushy tail, pointed muzzle, bright eyes, low posture, lean ribs.
Huge adult male Bengal tiger, great square head and massive shoulders, rich orange coat with bold black stripes, white ruffs, amber eyes; permanently lame in on
Indian human male: brown skin, long dark hair, hard bare feet; ages from naked soft dimpled baby just walking, to bare jungle child about 7, to lean athletic ne
Great gray Lone Wolf, pack leader: large mature male, full gray coat, heavier bone than Father Wolf, noble muzzle; ages to thinner, stiffer old wolf.
Young adult Seeonee wolf in fourth year, leaner and darker-mixed gray than Akela, intense eyes.
Large old brown sloth bear, heavy body, shaggy brown coat, blunt muzzle, small eyes, big padded paws, furry stomach.
Adult black panther (melanistic leopard), inky black coat with ghost rosettes visible as watered-silk sheen in light, sleek muscular body, soft fur, green eyes,
Troop of gray langur-like monkeys—long limbs, gray fur, black faces and hands, restless tails.
Large kite (bird of prey), brown and white underwings, hooked beak, very keen eyes, long wings.
Huge rock python about thirty feet, thick muscular coils, gleaming patterned scales, scarred nose, wedge head, bright eyes.
Indian cobras—hooded, scaled bodies; dusty ruin-dwelling snakes smaller than Kaa.
Young gray wolf, one of Mother Wolf’s litter grown—lean Seeonee gray coat, soft gray nose, pack-brother build.
Gaunt adult pack wolf, mangy tattered coat, shot-scarred or trap-lame look, hollow flanks.
Kind-faced Indian village woman, adult, warm brown skin, dark hair, soft maternal eyes; simple rural sari/cloth of a Waingunga-plain household.
Big fat Indian village priest, white clothes, red-and-yellow caste mark on forehead, heavy belly and jowls.
Adult Indian village hunter, lean weathered man with Tower musket, waist-cloth.
Crowd of North Indian plain villagers—men, women, children in dhotis, saris, turbans; herd-boys, yellow pariah dogs at edges.
Huge fifteen-year-old gray fur seal bull, nearly a mane on shoulders, long wicked dog-teeth, scarred all over from fights; stands over four feet on flippers, ab
Adult female fur seal, gentler softer bulk than Sea Catch, smooth dark coat, maternal eyes.
The White Seal: born with pale watery-blue eyes, large head and shoulders; grows to pure white fur, full-grown sea-catch bulk with curly white mane, heavy build
Young adult dark fur seal about Kotick’s age, beach-fighter bulk, dark coat without white.
Mass of fur seals—bulls, cows, holluschickie bachelors, pups—dark coats in dense groups on basalt and sand.
Great sea-cow (manatee-like), huge slow gray-brown body, calm face.
Small Indian mongoose, catlike fur, weasel-like head, pink eyes and restless pink nose, bottle-brush tail when roused, ivory fangs.
Small English boy in Segowlee cantonment, fair colonial child, period light clothes, big eyes.
Teddy’s Mother—English woman Alice, adult, kind face, period bungalow dress.
Big Englishman, adult male, period colonial shirt and trousers, solid paternal build; moustache optional.
Tailor-bird male, small songbird, olive-brown upperparts, pale underparts, long tail feathers.
Female tailor-bird, slightly duller small songbird, same species as Darzee.
Big black cobra, five feet long, spectacled hood spread, dull glowing eyes, thick body; spectacle mark on hood.
Adult female cobra, sleek hooded body, black-olive sheen matching Nag, intense eyes.
Muskrat, small brown wet-furred rodent, timid low posture along walls, whiskered pointed face.
Great Asian elephant nearly seventy years old; ten feet at the shoulder, dark wrinkled hide, tusks cut short at five feet and bound with copper bands; large eye
Weathered adult Indian mahout, dark sun-leathered skin, strong hands, working camp clothes; adult father build.
Indian boy ten years old, four feet high, sun-bleached brown hair flying loose, barefoot jungle-camp child in a single rag garment.
English Keddah chief, adult man, field mustache, solar-topee era colonial hunting dress.
Head tracker, adult Assamese/jungle Indian man, lean scarred hunter build, gun and field cloth.
Clever adult she-elephant, slightly smaller than Kala Nag, working government elephant, calm intelligent eye.
Young Indian woman in simple sari, soft maternal face.
Sharp fox terrier, white with brown markings, small dog carriage.
Adult camp man, period soldier-servant clothes, wet camp kit.
Tough screw-gun mule, straps rings and chains on saddle-pad, campaigner build; breech-piece mule of number two gun, First Screw Battery.
Large baggage camel of the 39th Native Infantry, soft feet, long bobbing neck, humped bulk.
Big Australian cavalry mount, Number Fifteen, E troop, Ninth Lancers; parade-fit, silky tail when groomed, one ear forward one back.
Pair of enormous patient gun-bullocks, heavy flanks, heavy yokes, slaty working cattle build.
Huge gun-team elephant, forty years’ service, harnessed to siege gun; trunk and dual-end bulk.
Amir of Afghanistan, Central Asian king, high big black astrakhan-wool hat, great diamond star, horseman in royal riding dress.

samples.visual: Petersen Sahib (smiling under his mustache).

Mowgli sleeps between Bagheera and Baloo, resolving to have no more to do with the Monkey People. Hard strong little hands seize his legs and arms. A swash of branches in his face. Baloo wakes the jungle with deep cries.…

Kaa finds a discolored crack in the marble tracery. Two or three light taps for distance-then six feet of body lifts clear and smashes home, nose-first, half a dozen full-power blows. Screen-work explodes in dust and rub…
Little Toomai leads Petersen Sahib and Machua Appa to the beaten ground-the dance-floor stamped hard as a road. The men stare. Petersen Sahib looks long at the boy.
He explores thoroughly-every beach, every shoal-so he can answer all questions. Then he dives, finds the tunnel mouth again, and races south.
Rikki snuffs at the boy's ear, climbs down, sits rubbing his nose.

Full moon. A hilltop of stones and boulders. AKELA, great gray Lone Wolf, lies full length on his rock. Below him, forty or more wolves of every size and color. Cubs tumble in the center. Senior wolves pad up, inspect, r…
Rikki-tikki explores: melon beds, roses, a thorn-bush, the long grass. He meets DARZEE the tailor-bird and his wife, fluttering in distress-a fledgling eaten.
The whine becomes a humming purr from every quarter-then the full-throated aaooughh:
The men roar with laughter. Little Toomai hangs eight feet up and wishes he were eight feet underground.
Baloo speaks.
Little Toomai speaks.

From habit the Pack answers. Lame wolves, shot-wounded wolves, mangy wolves-many missing-drag themselves to the Rock. They stare at the striped hide, huge claws dangling from empty feet. Mowgli leaps upon the rattling sk…

Bagheera runs to Mowgli's bare feet. They clamber up the Council Rock together. Mowgli spreads Shere Khan's striped hide on the flat stone where Akela used to sit, pegs it with four bamboo slivers. Akela lies down upon i…
Father Wolf at the mouth. Mother Wolf lifts her head.

Mowgli divides the herd-Akela taking the big bulls one way, Gray Brother the cows and calves the other-trapping Shere Khan, heavy with a village meal, in the narrow ravine. Buffaloes thunder. Dust. The lame tiger cannot …
Akela speaks.

Six days later. Kotick hauls out just above Sea Lion's Neck. The seal who has been waiting for him meets him first. She sees by the look in his eyes that he has found his island at last. The holluschickie, Sea Catch, and…
Narrator speaks.
Little Toomai speaks.

[YellowWallpaper] Two weeks in. THE NARRATOR at the window again, journal open. She is thinner in the face; the effort of dressing shows.
[YellowWallpaper] He takes her in his arms, fond, dismissing.
[YellowWallpaper] JENNIE's figure appears far down the winding road. THE NARRATOR hides the journal at once. JENNIE enters with clean linen, cheerful, thorough.
[YellowWallpaper] JOHN sits beside her, professional kindness.
[YellowWallpaper] She tries to speak earnestly. Tears come before she finishes.
[YellowWallpaper] He sits up straight-stern, reproachful. She stops.
[YellowWallpaper] Different light. THE NARRATOR watches the first long straight ray from the east window. The paper changes as she watches.
[YellowWallpaper] When he leaves, she sits up and stares at the wall again.
[YellowWallpaper] THE NARRATOR brighter, secret energy. She eats. JOHN laughs, relieved.
[YellowWallpaper] THE NARRATOR descends slowly. The yellow smell seems to wait on the treads. She touches the banister, then her hair.

[YellowWallpaper] From the nursery windows, THE NARRATOR's POV: a woman creeping in the long shaded lane; creeping in the dark grape arbors; creeping the long road under the trees-hiding under blackbe…
[YellowWallpaper] Last full night. JOHN stays in town. JENNIE offers to sleep in; THE NARRATOR refuses, sweet and firm.

[YellowWallpaper] Moving day tomorrow. Furniture already going downstairs. The room empties toward bareness: the great bedstead nailed down, canvas mattress left. JENNIE stares at the stripped wall in…
[YellowWallpaper] She fastens the rope-secures herself to the room's logic-and begins to creep, shoulder in the long smooch, round and round on the bare floor.
[YellowWallpaper] THE NARRATOR keeps creeping.
[YellowWallpaper] THE NARRATOR keeps creeping. She looks at him over her shoulder.

results.promptLabel: v2 grounded cut (time jump, fight energy, subject reframe)
results.note: From extend blind: baseline and v1 nearly agree; v2 targets mid-scene fight energy and new-subject scrambles baseline misses. Updated 2026-07-22: 3/4 models (grok-4.5, claude-sonnet-5, claude-fable-5) flipped s63_b35 ('a deep soft voice-the ELEPHANT of the gun-team') to hard_cut, over-applying the new-subject rule to a new voice joining an already-established group scene (verified against screenplay.fountain: this is one line in an ongoing multi-animal dialogue circle, same_location=true, prev also a speaker cue). Added a clarifying extend rule for that case; new-subject hard_cut now requires actual physical entrance/blocking, not just descriptive color on a voice.

samples.visual: From habit the Pack answers. Lame wolves, shot-wounded wolves, mangy wolves-many missing-drag themselves to the Rock. They stare at the striped hide, huge claws dangling from empty feet. Mowgli leaps …
Bushes rustle. Father Wolf drops on his haunches, ready to leap-then checks in mid-spring, shoots straight up four or five feet, and lands almost where he left ground.
Big Toomai speaks.
Kotick swims thousands of miles-year after year-searching. He asks sea birds, sharks, walruses. He visits beaches from California to Japan. Everywhere: seals, and the shadow of the sealers.
NARRATOR-a lean Englishman in shirt-sleeves, period moustache-jams on boots and waterproof. Vixen bolts out the other side.
Mud and drizzle. Heel-ropes snap. A mob of Afghan horses stampede through the dark between tent ropes. Camels break loose, blunder, fall over guy-lines. Men shout. Canvas heaves.
Baloo and Bagheera burst from the jungle. Monkeys scream and fling themselves upon them. Bagheera fights toward the tank; Baloo stands like a rock under a rain of biting, scratching bodies.

Year upon year, more seals leave Novastoshnah, Lukannon, and the other nurseries for the quiet sheltered beaches. Kotick sits all the summer through, getting bigger and fatter and stronger, while the …
Big Toomai speaks.

He has no time to change his mind. Kotick's head is out; teeth sink in the blubber of the young seal's neck. Kotick throws himself back on his haunches, hauls his enemy down the beach, shakes him, kno…
Toomai's Mother speaks.
Teddy's Father picks the mongoose up between finger and thumb.
Baloo speaks.
From the dark, a deep soft voice-the ELEPHANT of the gun-team, huge beyond the gun-wheels.
Father Wolf speaks.
Rain sheets down on a vast military camp-thirty thousand men, camels, elephants, horses, bullocks, and mules gathered for review. Mud roads, tent lines, gun parks. Flags hang heavy and wet.
Baloo speaks.
Mother Wolf speaks.
Father Wolf at the mouth. Mother Wolf lifts her head.
Mowgli, bare and scarred, jogs steadily along a rough road down the valley-twenty miles of dust and heat.
Toomai's Mother speaks.

Ruined city swallowed by jungle: broken domes, terraces, a great reservoir tank, marble summerhouse with pierced screen-work. Bandar-log pour over walls with Mowgli, chattering of kings and laws they …
Bagheera speaks.
Mowgli (scornfully).
